title: Waithe (2) Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Flt Sgt. Allen James Kevis

external_narratives.summary: Registry entry for the Waithe combined memorial commemorating four 100 Squadron Lancaster crews lost on Black Thursday. Lancaster JB674, piloted by F/Sgt Allen Kevis, collided with Lancaster JB678 at 00:40 on 17 December 1943 while both aircraft circled the fog-bound airfield.
external_narratives.key_details: JB674 is one of four 100 Squadron aircraft commemorated on the combined black marble memorial tablet. The collision occurred during the return from the Berlin raid in dense fog.

external_narratives.summary: Account of Black Thursday documenting the catastrophic losses at fog-bound airfields. Lancaster JB674 from 100 Squadron collided mid-air with JB678 after both had survived the Berlin raid, only to die in English fog waiting to land.
external_narratives.key_details: The collision of JB674 and JB678 was one of the cruelest ironies of Black Thursday — crews surviving the most dangerous target in Germany only to die within sight of their home airfield.

external_narratives.summary: 100 Squadron history page documenting the unit's WW2 formation, operations, and losses. Includes the Black Thursday losses among the squadron's operational record from RAF Grimsby (Waltham).
external_narratives.key_details: 100 Squadron operated from RAF Grimsby (also known as Waltham) as part of 1 Group, Bomber Command. The squadron suffered disproportionately heavy losses on 16/17 December 1943.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Postcodes.io — Waithe NE Lincolnshire DN37 at 53.487 -0.065 (same village as Waithe 1)
location_confidence.verification_notes: CORRECTION. Current coords (53.267, -0.209) ~24km south. Waithe village DN37 at 53.487, -0.065.
